text.title: Terse Default Tool Output Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns the response-envelope validator catches. Each pairs symptom + root cause + fix.

antipattern.symptom: Summary mode emits headline-only rows; agent has to re-call to discover IDs before escalating to `full`.
antipattern.root-cause: Author optimised the visible width of the table and dropped the id column to fit the terminal. The round-trip cost on the next call exceeded the saved tokens.
antipattern.fix: Make `id` the first column in every summary row. Validator rejects responses where any summary entry lacks an `id` field.

antipattern.symptom: Tool cuts result set to `limit` but returns no `truncated` flag and no `total_hits`.

root-cause.source: Harness — Agent loop OS; pagination postmortems

antipattern.root-cause: Author thought "limit" was a feature, not state. Model interprets "12 hits returned" as "12 hits exist" and stops searching.
antipattern.fix: Always return `truncated: bool` + `total_hits: int` (or upper-bound estimate). CI fails any tool whose response lacks both keys.

antipattern.symptom: Agent issues `format="full"` on every call "just to be safe". Average call cost climbs from 100 to 3000 tokens after deployment.
antipattern.root-cause: Tool description says "use summary for short, full for everything else" — wide opt-in clause invites the model to over-escalate.
antipattern.fix: Require a `reason` argument when `format="full"`. Eval gates raw-mode usage rate; alert if > 30% of calls request full without a logged reason.
